commands.review_summary:
The PR changes bounded transcript JSONL reads to hold the read stream in a variable and close both the readline interface and stream in a finally block.

PR surface: Source +6. Total +6 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: yes. from source, but not from a live runtime run: current main parses bounded transcript JSONL in a readline async loop where malformed JSON can throw before local cleanup. The PR body still does not include after-fix FD, log, or terminal proof for that path.

target.title: fix: close readline interface and destroy read stream in transcript store
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes a file descriptor leak: `createReadStream` + `readline.createInterface` were not cleaned up after `for await` loop exit.

## Why This Change Was Made

Wrap the loop in `try/finally` that calls `lines.close()` and `stream.destroy()`.

## User Impact

No user-visible change. Prevents file descriptor exhaustion.

## Evidence

```
src/agents/tools/transcripts-tool.test.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
     Tests  10 passed (10)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 20.13s
```

Node.js `readline` module does not close the underlying stream; explicit `close()` + `destroy()` is the recommended cleanup pattern.
target.author: zhanglei99586
